charity.amt: Not disclosed annually; $1M pledged to FoodCorps over 2 years
charity.focus: Food insecurity, school nutrition, community food access

wiki.controversies:
== Controversies==
In 2023 leaders of Sweetgreens weighed in on the Gaza war protests at universities stating their company views was that the names of students involved in the protests should be released so that Sweetgreens does not inadvertently hire any signers of perceived anti Israel petitions signed by students.

In 2026 Sweetgreen announced they were collaborating with anti-vaxxer Dr. Mark Hyman to update their menu.
wiki.extract:
Sweetgreen (legally Sweetgreen, Inc., stylized as sweetgreen, previously swɘetgreen) is an American fast casual restaurant chain that serves salads. It was founded in November 2006 by Nicolas Jammet, Nathaniel Ru, and Jonathan Neman. In August 2007, they opened their first store in Washington, D.C., three months after they graduated from the McDonough School of Business at Georgetown University.
Sweetgreen's corporate headquarters moved to the Los Angeles area from Washington, D.C., in 2016. As of May 2025, it had 251 stores in operation in 24 states and the District of Columbia. The CEO is Jo
